keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Supply Chain Security platform engineered to an automated metadata vulnerability scanner that catalogs dependencies and monitors container images for threats..
Integrates Artifact Analysis capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the DevOps Continuous Integration and Application Delivery portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Automates software builds, containerization, and deployments using declaration files (YAML/JSON).
Supports release automation with built-in progressive rollout strategies (canary, blue-green, and rolling upgrades).
Secures the software supply chain by scanning dependencies, code, and container images for known vulnerabilities.
Optimizes Artifact Analysis performance through automated platform scaling and resource balancing.
Enables resource segregation, logical naming conventions, and tag-based configuration policies for Artifact Analysis.
Integrates with VPC routing and private service access architectures to secure communication tunnels.
Governs access to Artifact Analysis using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/artifactanalysis.admin and roles/artifactanalysis.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Artifact Analysis that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Artifact Analysis resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Artifact Analysis operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Artifact Analysis resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Artifact Analysis resource
commands.command:
gcloud artifacts repositories create prod-artifactanalysis-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Artifact Analysis Resource"

commands.description: List active Artifact Analysis instances and query configuration states
commands.command: gcloud artifacts repositories list --format="table(name, state, updateTime)"
